<!-- Change Proposal: Add BLAKE3 to HashNameVocab -->
<!-- Target repository: UCO -->
<!-- Target release: 1.5.0 -->
<!-- Generated by CASE/UCO SDK draft_change_proposal tool, refined by Cory Hall on behalf of Project VIC International -->

# Target release

**Target**: UCO 1.5.0

This is a backward-compatible addition: appending one new literal (`"BLAKE3"`) to an existing open vocabulary (`vocabulary:HashNameVocab`) and to the matching `sh:in` enumeration on the `types:Hash` `hashMethod` SHACL property shape. No existing graph becomes invalid as a result of this change.

# Background

UCO's `vocabulary:HashNameVocab` (declared in `ontology/uco/vocabulary/vocabulary.ttl`) and the matching `sh:in` enumeration on the `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` SHACL property shape (declared in `ontology/uco/types/types.ttl`) currently enumerate twelve hashing algorithm names: MD5, MD6, SHA1, SHA224, SHA256, SHA3-224, SHA3-256, SHA3-384, SHA3-512, SHA384, SHA512, and SSDEEP. Neither enumeration includes BLAKE3.

[BLAKE3](https://github.com/BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3) is a cryptographic hash function with a stable, community-recognized canonical name established by its [official specification](https://github.com/BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3-specs/blob/master/blake3.pdf) (Aumasson, Neves, Wilcox-O'Hearn, O'Connor, 2020) and reference implementations. The reference codebase and CLI (`b3sum`, packaged in current Debian, Ubuntu, Fedora, and Homebrew repositories) have been published since 2020 under permissive licences. The strongest, easiest-to-cite evidence that BLAKE3 is showing up in real CASE/UCO bundles today is:

- **Project VIC International — `media-authentication`** uses BLAKE3 as the default integrity hash in its evidence-ingest pipeline (`src/media_auth/pipeline/steps/evidence.py`) and emits it as the `hashMethod` value of every `types:Hash` attached to a file's `ContentDataFacet`. Bundles signed by this pipeline ship to downstream review tools and are the originating motivation for this proposal (see issue [#75](https://github.com/Project-VIC-International/media-authentication/issues/75)).
- **The BLAKE3 reference implementation and `b3sum` CLI** ([BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3](https://github.com/BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3)) — establishes the canonical algorithm name and digest formatting that any forensic tool computing a BLAKE3 hash will use when populating a `hashMethod` literal.

Because BLAKE3 is not in the upstream vocabulary today, every CASE/UCO bundle that records a BLAKE3 integrity hash currently produces an `sh:Info`-severity finding from `case_validate`:

```
Value is not member of the vocabulary HashNameVocab.
sh:focusNode kb:hash-...
sh:resultPath types:hashMethod
sh:value "BLAKE3"
```

`HashNameVocab` is documented as an **open vocabulary** (`rdfs:comment "Defines an open-vocabulary of hashing algorithm names."`), so this finding is informational rather than a violation. Project VIC works around it today by declaring a project-local `urn:media-auth:ext:HashNameExtensionVocab` in `src/media_auth/case/shapes/media-auth-ext.ttl` (closes Project VIC issue [#69](https://github.com/Project-VIC-International/media-authentication/issues/69), per spec `029-shacl-conformance-fix`) so the validator routes the finding to `info` and not `violations`. That extension is maintenance debt — every consumer that wants to interpret `BLAKE3` as a first-class algorithm needs to either know about the local extension or live with an unexplained Info finding.

The argument for adding `BLAKE3` upstream is an **ontology-interoperability** argument, not an endorsement of algorithm quality or standardization status. The vocabulary is open and is already accumulating `BLAKE3` literals from real bundles; recognising the canonical literal upstream lets validators and downstream consumers stop emitting (and stop having to interpret) avoidable informational noise, and it removes the per-consumer pressure to invent a local extension to describe the same value.

## Related proposals and references

- **Project VIC International issue #75** — [ontology: contribute BLAKE3 to UCO HashNameVocab upstream](https://github.com/Project-VIC-International/media-authentication/issues/75). The originating request that prompted this proposal. Project VIC has committed to retiring `ma-ext:BLAKE3` from `media-auth-ext.ttl` and bumping the pinned `uco-ontology` version once this proposal merges upstream.
- **BLAKE3 specification** — [The BLAKE3 paper](https://github.com/BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3-specs/blob/master/blake3.pdf) (Aumasson, Neves, Wilcox-O'Hearn, O'Connor, 2020). The canonical citation for the algorithm and its security properties.
- **BLAKE3 reference implementations** — [BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3](https://github.com/BLAKE3-team/BLAKE3) (Rust + Python + C reference, plus the `b3sum` CLI). The community-maintained reference codebase that establishes the canonical algorithm name and output formatting.
- **Naming-precedent analogy** — the existing SHA-3 family entries in `HashNameVocab` correspond to canonical published names; for SHA-3 those names come from [NIST FIPS 202](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.202.pdf) (the SHA-3 standard itself; SP 800-185 covers SHA-3-derived functions like cSHAKE, KMAC, TupleHash, and ParallelHash, which are not in `HashNameVocab` and are out of scope here). BLAKE3 lacks a NIST publication, but it does have a stable, community-recognized canonical name in its official specification and reference implementation, which is the property the vocabulary actually relies on.

# Requirements

## Requirement 1: Add `"BLAKE3"` to `vocabulary:HashNameVocab`

In `ontology/uco/vocabulary/vocabulary.ttl`, add the literal `"BLAKE3"` to the `owl:oneOf` list inside the `owl:equivalentClass` blank node of `vocabulary:HashNameVocab`. The canonical case is `BLAKE3` (uppercase, no dash), matching the upstream spec, the `b3sum` output prefix, and the existing all-caps/no-dash style used for `MD5`, `SHA1`, `SHA256`, etc. (`SHA3-224` etc. retain a dash because [NIST FIPS 202](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.202.pdf) names the family "SHA-3" with a digest-length suffix; BLAKE3 has no analogous in-family suffix.)

## Requirement 2: Add `"BLAKE3"` to the `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` `sh:in` list

In `ontology/uco/types/types.ttl`, add `"BLAKE3"` to the `sh:in` list of the `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` SHACL property shape so that `case_validate` no longer emits an `sh:Info`-severity 'Value is not member of the vocabulary HashNameVocab' finding when a `types:Hash` records a BLAKE3 hash.

# Risk / Benefit analysis

## Benefits

- **Removes a documented workaround** in at least one production CSAM-investigation pipeline (Project VIC `media-authentication`), allowing it to drop a project-local `HashNameExtensionVocab` and pin a clean upstream version.
- **Eliminates spurious `sh:Info` findings** for every CASE/UCO bundle that records a BLAKE3 integrity hash, improving the signal-to-noise ratio of `case_validate` output.
- **Reduces per-consumer extension pressure**: any `BLAKE3` literal already appearing in a real bundle can be interpreted against the upstream vocabulary alone, without consulting per-vendor extension documentation. This is consistent with how the open vocabulary already accepts other community-canonical names.
- **Trivial implementation cost**: two single-line additions, no new classes, no new properties, no new shapes, no breaking changes.

## Risks

- **Backward compatibility**: None. `HashNameVocab` is documented as an open vocabulary (`owl:equivalentClass [ a rdfs:Datatype ; owl:oneOf ( ... ) ]`) and `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` already uses `sh:severity sh:Info`, so any existing graph remains valid before and after the change. Graphs that already record `BLAKE3` simply stop producing the `Info` finding.
- **Vocabulary churn**: Future hash algorithms (e.g., a hypothetical `BLAKE3s`, post-quantum algorithm proposals) may also want admission. The `HashNameVocab` enumeration has historically grown by one or two members per release cycle and there is no evidence this rate is unsustainable.
- **Naming style**: This proposal uses the canonical `BLAKE3` (uppercase, no dash) to match the upstream spec and existing `MD5`/`SHA256` style. The committee should confirm whether this is preferred over alternatives (`Blake3`, `blake3`); the originating issue explicitly recommends "BLAKE3 (canonical case, no dash)".

# Competencies demonstrated

## Scenario

A CSAM-investigation lab uses the [Project VIC International `media-authentication`](https://github.com/Project-VIC-International/media-authentication) pipeline to ingest seized media, compute integrity hashes, and emit signed CASE/UCO bundles for downstream review tools. The pipeline computes BLAKE3 as its primary integrity hash for every evidence file and records it as a `types:Hash` on the file's `ContentDataFacet`. Pipelines may additionally record other hashes (e.g., SHA-256) per local policy; this proposal does not prescribe that choice.

After this change merges, the lab can drop its local `HashNameExtensionVocab`, the `sh:Info` 'not in HashNameVocab' findings disappear from validation output, and any CASE/UCO consumer can reason about BLAKE3 hashes directly against the upstream vocabulary.

### Objects in the example graph

- **`kb:file-1`** — an `ObservableObject` representing a seized JPEG, with a `FileFacet` (filename, size) and a `ContentDataFacet` carrying two `types:Hash` instances:
  - `kb:hash-blake3-1` — `hashMethod = "BLAKE3"`, `hashValue` = the 256-bit BLAKE3 digest.
  - `kb:hash-sha256-1` — `hashMethod = "SHA256"`, `hashValue` = the 256-bit SHA-256 digest.
- **`kb:file-2`** — a second `ObservableObject` whose `ContentDataFacet` records a `BLAKE3` hash. This proposal is neutral on whether implementations also record additional hashes such as SHA-256; this example simply demonstrates that a graph may record `BLAKE3` independently from a vocabulary-validation standpoint.

### Competency Question 1

**Question**: Which `types:Hash` instances in the bundle were computed with BLAKE3?

#### Result 1

A SPARQL query selecting `?hash` and `?hashValue` where `?hash a types:Hash ; types:hashMethod "BLAKE3"` returns both `kb:hash-blake3-1` and `kb:hash-blake3-2`. Today this query already returns the correct results, but `case_validate` emits an `sh:Info` finding for each BLAKE3 hash. After the change, the same query returns the same results with no informational findings.

### Competency Question 2

**Question**: Which evidence files have at least one BLAKE3 integrity hash recorded?

#### Result 2

A SPARQL query traversing `ObservableObject` → `hasFacet` → `ContentDataFacet` → `hash` → `Hash[hashMethod="BLAKE3"]` returns `kb:file-1` and `kb:file-2`.

### Draft SPARQL

```sparql
PREFIX uco-core: <https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/core/>
PREFIX uco-observable: <https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/observable/>
PREFIX types: <https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/types/>

SELECT ?file ?hash ?hashValue
WHERE {
    ?file a uco-observable:ObservableObject ;
          uco-core:hasFacet ?cdf .
    ?cdf  a uco-observable:ContentDataFacet ;
          uco-observable:hash ?hash .
    ?hash a types:Hash ;
          types:hashMethod "BLAKE3" ;
          types:hashValue ?hashValue .
}
```

# Example instance data

The example graph is also available as a standalone file at `change_proposals/add-blake3-to-hashnamevocab.jsonld` for validation and SPARQL testing.

```json
{
  "@context": {
    "kb": "http://example.org/kb/",
    "rdfs": "http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#",
    "uco-core": "https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/core/",
    "uco-observable": "https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/observable/",
    "types": "https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/types/",
    "vocabulary": "https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/vocabulary/",
    "xsd": "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#"
  },
  "@graph": [
    {
      "@id": "kb:file-1",
      "@type": "uco-observable:ObservableObject",
      "uco-core:hasFacet": [
        {
          "@id": "kb:file-1-file-facet",
          "@type": "uco-observable:FileFacet",
          "uco-observable:fileName": "exhibit-001.jpg",
          "uco-observable:sizeInBytes": 4096
        },
        {
          "@id": "kb:file-1-contentdata-facet",
          "@type": "uco-observable:ContentDataFacet",
          "uco-observable:hash": [
            { "@id": "kb:hash-blake3-1" },
            { "@id": "kb:hash-sha256-1" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:hash-blake3-1",
      "@type": "types:Hash",
      "rdfs:comment": "Post-change: no sh:Info finding. Pre-change: triggers sh:Info because 'BLAKE3' is not in the current upstream HashNameVocab.",
      "types:hashMethod": "BLAKE3",
      "types:hashValue": {
        "@type": "xsd:hexBinary",
        "@value": "af1349b9f5f9a1a6a0404dea36dcc9499bcb25c9adc112b7cc9a93cae41f3262"
      }
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:hash-sha256-1",
      "@type": "types:Hash",
      "rdfs:comment": "Recorded alongside the BLAKE3 hash. This proposal is neutral on whether implementations record one hash or several; the example only demonstrates that both algorithm literals are vocabulary-recognised post-change.",
      "types:hashMethod": "SHA256",
      "types:hashValue": {
        "@type": "xsd:hexBinary",
        "@value": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"
      }
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:file-2",
      "@type": "uco-observable:ObservableObject",
      "uco-core:hasFacet": [
        {
          "@id": "kb:file-2-file-facet",
          "@type": "uco-observable:FileFacet",
          "uco-observable:fileName": "exhibit-002.mp4",
          "uco-observable:sizeInBytes": 1048576
        },
        {
          "@id": "kb:file-2-contentdata-facet",
          "@type": "uco-observable:ContentDataFacet",
          "uco-observable:hash": [
            { "@id": "kb:hash-blake3-2" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:hash-blake3-2",
      "@type": "types:Hash",
      "rdfs:comment": "Demonstrates that a graph may record BLAKE3 independently. This proposal is neutral on whether implementations also record additional hashes such as SHA-256.",
      "types:hashMethod": "BLAKE3",
      "types:hashValue": {
        "@type": "xsd:hexBinary",
        "@value": "2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

I am fine with my examples being transcribed and credited.

# Solution suggestion

* In `ontology/uco/vocabulary/vocabulary.ttl`, add `"BLAKE3"` to the `owl:oneOf` list of `vocabulary:HashNameVocab`. Suggested placement is alphabetical (before `MD5`):

  ```turtle
  vocabulary:HashNameVocab
      a rdfs:Datatype ;
      rdfs:label "Hash Name Vocabulary"@en-US ;
      rdfs:comment "Defines an open-vocabulary of hashing algorithm names."@en-US ;
      owl:equivalentClass [
          a rdfs:Datatype ;
          owl:oneOf (
              "BLAKE3"
              "MD5"
              "MD6"
              "SHA1"
              "SHA224"
              "SHA256"
              "SHA3-224"
              "SHA3-256"
              "SHA3-384"
              "SHA3-512"
              "SHA384"
              "SHA512"
              "SSDEEP"
          ) ;
      ] ;
      .
  ```

* In `ontology/uco/types/types.ttl`, add `"BLAKE3"` to the `sh:in` list of `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` so the SHACL `Info`-severity finding stops firing for compliant graphs:

  ```turtle
  types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape
      a sh:PropertyShape ;
      sh:in (
          "BLAKE3"
          "MD5"
          "MD6"
          "SHA1"
          "SHA224"
          "SHA256"
          "SHA3-224"
          "SHA3-256"
          "SHA3-384"
          "SHA3-512"
          "SHA384"
          "SHA512"
          "SSDEEP"
      ) ;
      sh:message "Value is not member of the vocabulary HashNameVocab." ;
      sh:path types:hashMethod ;
      sh:severity sh:Info ;
      .
  ```

* Add a positive test case to `tests/examples/hash_PASS.json` (or its equivalent) showing a `types:Hash` with `hashMethod = "BLAKE3"` validating without an `sh:Info` finding, paired with a comment indicating the algorithm reference.
* No SDK migration is required: existing graphs that already use `"BLAKE3"` simply stop producing the informational warning. No SHACL violation will be introduced.

# Pre-submission testing

`make test-proposal PROPOSAL=add-blake3-to-hashnamevocab` was run against `case-1.4.0` (the current pinned build version in the SDK Makefile) with `--inference rdfs --allow-info`. All checks pass.

Pre-submission testing was deliberately performed against the current baseline (`case-1.4.0`) to confirm the **pre-change** behavior and isolate the existing `sh:Info` findings that this proposal eliminates. Post-merge verification should be rerun against the branch or release candidate containing the proposed updates (e.g., a `develop` build with the two single-line additions applied) to confirm that the two `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` `Info` findings disappear and only the unrelated cosmetic UUID-suggestion findings remain.

## SPARQL query testing

| Query | Tested | Expected results match | Notes |
|-------|--------|----------------------|-------|
| CQ 1 — `BLAKE3` hashes | Yes | Yes | 2 results — `kb:hash-blake3-1`, `kb:hash-blake3-2`. |
| CQ 2 — files with a BLAKE3 hash | Yes | Yes | 2 results — `kb:file-1`, `kb:file-2`. |
| CQ 3 — every (algorithm, hashValue) pair per file | Yes | Yes | 3 results — `kb:file-1` × {BLAKE3, SHA256}, `kb:file-2` × {BLAKE3}. |

## Graph validation

```
$ make validate-proposal PROPOSAL=add-blake3-to-hashnamevocab
=== Validating proposal graph: change_proposals/add-blake3-to-hashnamevocab.jsonld ===
Validation Report
Conforms: True
Results (11):
  - 2 × sh:Info  types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape
                 "Value is not member of the vocabulary HashNameVocab."
                 (kb:hash-blake3-1, kb:hash-blake3-2)
  - 9 × sh:Info  core:UcoThing-identifier-regex-shape
                 "UcoThings are suggested to end with a UUID."
                 (cosmetic IRI-style suggestion; non-blocking)
```

The graph **conforms** under the current pre-change `case-1.4.0` build because `HashNameVocab` is documented as an open vocabulary and `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` uses `sh:severity sh:Info` rather than `sh:Violation`. The two `Info`-severity findings about `BLAKE3` are exactly the spurious findings this proposal eliminates: once `"BLAKE3"` is added to both the `vocabulary:HashNameVocab` `owl:oneOf` list and the `types:Hash-hashMethod-in-shape` `sh:in` list, those two findings disappear and the validation report shrinks to the 9 cosmetic UUID-suggestion findings (which are unrelated to this proposal and would be addressed by switching the example IRIs to UUID form).

## Unresolved issues

- **Naming style** — confirm `BLAKE3` (uppercase, no dash) is the preferred canonical form. The originating Project VIC issue explicitly recommends this form, matching `b3sum` and the upstream spec.
- **SHA-3 family parity** — the SHA-3 entries use a dash (`SHA3-256`) because [NIST FIPS 202](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.202.pdf) names the family "SHA-3" and distinguishes by digest length within the family; BLAKE3 has no analogous in-family digest-length variant, so `BLAKE3` (no dash) is consistent with the per-algorithm naming convention used by `MD5`, `SHA256`, `SSDEEP`.
